raw_consumer_statement:
XXXX XXXX, XXXX, I paid {$5.00} to XXXX XXXX, to talk to a doctor about my brother 's XXXX XXXX. After speaking to the doctor, I then received an email that I was charged {$46.00} per month for their services. I immediately contacted XXXX, and told them I was never informed of this amount. They cancelled, and credited the amount of {$46.00}. I disputed the amount with American Express as it was still in my account on XXXX XXXX, XXXX. They said they credited my account. 
Each statement since then, still has the {$46.00} charge on it. I have always paid in full for the charges that I did owe on, minus the {$46.00}. Now as of XXXX, XXXX, I am being charged late fees on top of the {$46.00} that was supposed to be credited. I chatted with AMEX in XXXX several times. One supervisor even said he agreed that I only owed two charges that I agreed I would pay for. I called AMEX corporate headquarters XXXX XXXX, XXXX in XXXX XXXX XXXX. They transferred me to their XXXX  office where XXXX said he would take it off my account, but it's still there. XXXX XXXX, XXXX, I filed a complaint with the XXXX. For some reason nobody at American Express can get it through their heads that I don't owe {$46.00} plus late fees. Today, XXXX XXXX, XXXX, I received an email threatening to send me to the credit bureau for not paying. I have now received a letter from AMERICAN Express that they can't credit me late fees. The XXXX is NOT helping at all. I was informed today, the guy who was in charge of my account no longer works for the XXXX. If they credited my account, why is it still on my account as being owed with late fees, and taking me to the credit bureau? I have never had problems with them until now. I have always had an excellent credit rating, and I don't want this company to ruin that for me. I no longer want this card, and will not be using it again.
